verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est verbum Domini ad me, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֥י דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֖ה אֵלַ֥י לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.king_james: Moreover the word of the Lord came to me, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Vade, et clama in auribus Jerusalem, dicens :  Hæc dicit Dominus :  Recordatus sum tui, miserans adolescentiam tuam,  et caritatem desponsationis tuæ,  quando secuta es me in deserto,  in terra quæ non seminatur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הָלֹ֡ךְ וְֽקָרָ֩אתָ֩ בְאׇזְנֵ֨י יְרוּשָׁלַ֜͏ִם לֵאמֹ֗ר כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה זָכַ֤רְתִּי לָךְ֙ חֶ֣סֶד נְעוּרַ֔יִךְ אַהֲבַ֖ת כְּלוּלֹתָ֑יִךְ לֶכְתֵּ֤ךְ אַֽחֲרַי֙ בַּמִּדְבָּ֔ר בְּאֶ֖רֶץ לֹ֥א זְרוּעָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Go and cry in the ears of Jerusalem, saying, Thus saith the Lord; I remember thee, the kindness of thy youth, the love of thine espousals, when thou wentest after me in the wilderness, in a land that was not sown.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sanctus Israël Domino,  primitiæ frugum ejus :  omnes qui devorant eum delinquunt :  mala venient super eos,  dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קֹ֤דֶשׁ יִשְׂרָאֵל֙ לַיהֹוָ֔ה רֵאשִׁ֖ית תְּבוּאָתֹ֑ה כׇּל־אֹכְלָ֣יו יֶאְשָׁ֔מוּ רָעָ֛ה תָּבֹ֥א אֲלֵיהֶ֖ם נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Israel was holiness unto the Lord, and the firstfruits of his increase: all that devour him shall offend; evil shall come upon them, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audite verbum Domini, domus Jacob,  et omnes cognationes domus Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שִׁמְע֥וּ דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֖ה בֵּ֣ית יַעֲקֹ֑ב וְכׇֽל־מִשְׁפְּח֖וֹת בֵּ֥ית יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Hear ye the word of the Lord, O house of Jacob, and all the families of the house of Israel:

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus :  Quid invenerunt patres vestri in me iniquitatis,  quia elongaverunt a me,  et ambulaverunt post vanitatem,  et vani facti sunt ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֣ה ׀ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה מַה־מָּצְא֨וּ אֲבוֹתֵיכֶ֥ם בִּי֙ עָ֔וֶל כִּ֥י רָחֲק֖וּ מֵעָלָ֑י וַיֵּ֥לְכ֛וּ אַחֲרֵ֥י הַהֶ֖בֶל וַיֶּהְבָּֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord, What iniquity have your fathers found in me, that they are gone far from me, and have walked after vanity, and are become vain?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et non dixerunt : Ubi est Dominus  qui ascendere nos fecit de terra Ægypti ;  qui traduxit nos per desertum,  per terram inhabitabilem et inviam,  per terram sitis, et imaginem mortis,  per terram in qua non ambulavit vir,  neque habitavit homo ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹ֣א אָֽמְר֔וּ אַיֵּ֣ה יְהֹוָ֔ה הַמַּעֲלֶ֥ה אֹתָ֖נוּ מֵאֶ֣רֶץ מִצְרָ֑יִם הַמּוֹלִ֨יךְ אֹתָ֜נוּ בַּמִּדְבָּ֗ר בְּאֶ֨רֶץ עֲרָבָ֤ה וְשׁוּחָה֙ בְּאֶ֙רֶץ֙ צִיָּ֣ה וְצַלְמָ֔וֶת בְּאֶ֗רֶץ לֹא־עָ֤בַר בָּהּ֙ אִ֔ישׁ וְלֹא־יָשַׁ֥ב אָדָ֖ם שָֽׁם׃
verses.king_james: Neither said they, Where is the Lord that brought us up out of the land of Egypt, that led us through the wilderness, through a land of deserts and of pits, through a land of drought, and of the shadow of death, through a land that no man passed through, and where no man dwelt?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et induxi vos in terram Carmeli,  ut comederetis fructum ejus et optima illius :  et ingressi contaminastis terram meam,  et hæreditatem meam posuistis in abominationem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וָאָבִ֤יא אֶתְכֶם֙ אֶל־אֶ֣רֶץ הַכַּרְמֶ֔ל לֶאֱכֹ֥ל פִּרְיָ֖הּ וְטוּבָ֑הּ וַתָּבֹ֙אוּ֙ וַתְּטַמְּא֣וּ אֶת־אַרְצִ֔י וְנַחֲלָתִ֥י שַׂמְתֶּ֖ם לְתוֹעֵבָֽה׃
verses.king_james: And I brought you into a plentiful country, to eat the fruit thereof and the goodness thereof; but when ye entered, ye defiled my land, and made mine heritage an abomination.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sacerdotes non dixerunt : Ubi est Dominus ?  et tenentes legem nescierunt me,  et pastores prævaricati sunt in me,  et prophetæ prophetaverunt in Baal,  et idola secuti sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַכֹּהֲנִ֗ים לֹ֤א אָֽמְרוּ֙ אַיֵּ֣ה יְהֹוָ֔ה וְתֹפְשֵׂ֤י הַתּוֹרָה֙ לֹ֣א יְדָע֔וּנִי וְהָרֹעִ֖ים פָּ֣שְׁעוּ בִ֑י וְהַנְּבִאִים֙ נִבְּא֣וּ בַבַּ֔עַל וְאַחֲרֵ֥י לֹֽא־יוֹעִ֖לוּ הָלָֽכוּ׃
verses.king_james: The priests said not, Where is the Lord? and they that handle the law knew me not: the pastors also transgressed against me, and the prophets prophesied by Baal, and walked after things that do not profit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea adhuc judicio contendam vobiscum, ait Dominus,  et cum filiis vestris disceptabo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֗ן עֹ֛ד אָרִ֥יב אִתְּכֶ֖ם נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה וְאֶת־בְּנֵ֥י בְנֵיכֶ֖ם אָרִֽיב׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore I will yet plead with you, saith the Lord, and with your children’s children will I plead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Transite ad insulas Cethim, et videte :  et in Cedar mittite, et considerate vehementer :  et videte si factum est hujuscemodi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י עִבְר֞וּ אִיֵּ֤י כִתִּיִּים֙ וּרְא֔וּ וְקֵדָ֛ר שִׁלְח֥וּ וְהִֽתְבּוֹנְנ֖וּ מְאֹ֑ד וּרְא֕וּ הֵ֥ן הָיְתָ֖ה כָּזֹֽאת׃
verses.king_james: For pass over the isles of Chittim, and see; and send unto Kedar, and consider diligently, and see if there be such a thing.

verses.latin_vulgate: si mutavit gens deos suos,  et certe ipsi non sunt dii :  populus vero meus mutavit gloriam suam in idolum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַהֵימִ֥יר גּוֹי֙ אֱלֹהִ֔ים וְהֵ֖מָּה לֹ֣א אֱלֹהִ֑ים וְעַמִּ֛י הֵמִ֥יר כְּבוֹד֖וֹ בְּל֥וֹא יוֹעִֽיל׃
verses.king_james: Hath a nation changed their gods, which are yet no gods? but my people have changed their glory for that which doth not profit.

verses.latin_vulgate: Obstupescite, cæli, super hoc,  et portæ ejus, desolamini vehementer, dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שֹׁ֥מּוּ שָׁמַ֖יִם עַל־זֹ֑את וְשַׂעֲר֛וּ חׇרְב֥וּ מְאֹ֖ד נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Be astonished, O ye heavens, at this, and be horribly afraid, be ye very desolate, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Duo enim mala fecit populus meus :  me dereliquerunt fontem aquæ vivæ,  et foderunt sibi cisternas, cisternas dissipatas,  quia continere non valent aquas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־שְׁתַּ֥יִם רָע֖וֹת עָשָׂ֣ה עַמִּ֑י אֹתִ֨י עָזְב֜וּ מְק֣וֹר ׀ מַ֣יִם חַיִּ֗ים לַחְצֹ֤ב לָהֶם֙ בֹּאר֔וֹת בֹּארֹת֙ נִשְׁבָּרִ֔ים אֲשֶׁ֥ר לֹֽא־יָכִ֖לוּ הַמָּֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: For my people have committed two evils; they have forsaken me the fountain of living waters, and hewed them out cisterns, broken cisterns, that can hold no water.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid servus est Israël, aut vernaculus ?  quare ergo factus est in prædam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַעֶ֙בֶד֙ יִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אִם־יְלִ֥יד בַּ֖יִת ה֑וּא מַדּ֖וּעַ הָיָ֥ה לָבַֽז׃
verses.king_james: Is Israel a servant? is he a homeborn slave? why is he spoiled?

verses.latin_vulgate: Super eum rugierunt leones,  et dederunt vocem suam :  posuerunt terram ejus in solitudinem.  Civitates ejus exustæ sunt,  et non est qui habitet in eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָלָיו֙ יִשְׁאֲג֣וּ כְפִרִ֔ים נָתְנ֖וּ קוֹלָ֑ם וַיָּשִׁ֤יתוּ אַרְצוֹ֙ לְשַׁמָּ֔ה עָרָ֥יו (נצתה) [נִצְּת֖וּ] מִבְּלִ֥י יֹשֵֽׁב׃
verses.king_james: The young lions roared upon him, and yelled, and they made his land waste: his cities are burned without inhabitant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filii quoque Mempheos et Taphnes  constupraverunt te usque ad verticem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּם־בְּנֵי־נֹ֖ף (ותחפנס) [וְתַחְפַּנְחֵ֑ס] יִרְע֖וּךְ קׇדְקֹֽד׃
verses.king_james: Also the children of Noph and Tahapanes have broken the crown of thy head.

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid non istud factum est tibi,  quia dereliquisti Dominum Deum tuum  eo tempore quo ducebat te per viam ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלוֹא־זֹ֖את תַּעֲשֶׂה־לָּ֑ךְ עׇזְבֵךְ֙ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהַ֔יִךְ בְּעֵ֖ת מוֹלִכֵ֥ךְ בַּדָּֽרֶךְ׃
verses.king_james: Hast thou not procured this unto thyself, in that thou hast forsaken the Lord thy God, when he led thee by the way?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc quid tibi vis in via Ægypti,  ut bibas aquam turbidam ?  et quid tibi cum via Assyriorum,  ut bibas aquam fluminis ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְעַתָּ֗ה מַה־לָּךְ֙ לְדֶ֣רֶךְ מִצְרַ֔יִם לִשְׁתּ֖וֹת מֵ֣י שִׁח֑וֹר וּמַה־לָּךְ֙ לְדֶ֣רֶךְ אַשּׁ֔וּר לִשְׁתּ֖וֹת מֵ֥י נָהָֽר׃
verses.king_james: And now what hast thou to do in the way of Egypt, to drink the waters of Sihor? or what hast thou to do in the way of Assyria, to drink the waters of the river?

verses.latin_vulgate: Arguet te malitia tua,  et aversio tua increpabit te.  Scito et vide quia malum et amarum est  reliquisse te Dominum Deum tuum,  et non esse timorem mei apud te,  dicit Dominus Deus exercituum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תְּיַסְּרֵ֣ךְ רָעָתֵ֗ךְ וּמְשֻׁבוֹתַ֙יִךְ֙ תּֽוֹכִחֻ֔ךְ וּדְעִ֤י וּרְאִי֙ כִּֽי־רַ֣ע וָמָ֔ר עׇזְבֵ֖ךְ אֶת־יְהֹוָ֣ה אֱלֹהָ֑יִךְ וְלֹ֤א פַחְדָּתִי֙ אֵלַ֔יִךְ נְאֻם־אֲדֹנָ֥י יֱהֹוִ֖ה צְבָאֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: Thine own wickedness shall correct thee, and thy backslidings shall reprove thee: know therefore and see that it is an evil thing and bitter, that thou hast forsaken the Lord thy God, and that my fear is not in thee, saith the Lord God of hosts.

verses.latin_vulgate: A sæculo confregisti jugum meum :  rupisti vincula mea,  et dixisti : Non serviam.  In omni enim colle sublimi,  et sub omni ligno frondoso,  tu prosternebaris meretrix.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י מֵעוֹלָ֞ם שָׁבַ֣רְתִּי עֻלֵּ֗ךְ נִתַּ֙קְתִּי֙ מוֹסְרוֹתַ֔יִךְ וַתֹּאמְרִ֖י לֹ֣א (אעבוד) [אֶעֱב֑וֹר] כִּ֣י עַֽל־כׇּל־גִּבְעָ֞ה גְּבֹהָ֗ה וְתַ֙חַת֙ כׇּל־עֵ֣ץ רַעֲנָ֔ן אַ֖תְּ צֹעָ֥ה זֹנָֽה׃
verses.king_james: For of old time I have broken thy yoke, and burst thy bands; and thou saidst, I will not transgress; when upon every high hill and under every green tree thou wanderest, playing the harlot.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego autem plantavi te vineam electam,  omne semen verum :  quomodo ergo conversa es mihi in pravum,  vinea aliena ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאָֽנֹכִי֙ נְטַעְתִּ֣יךְ שׂוֹרֵ֔ק כֻּלֹּ֖ה זֶ֣רַע אֱמֶ֑ת וְאֵיךְ֙ נֶהְפַּ֣כְתְּ לִ֔י סוּרֵ֖י הַגֶּ֥פֶן נׇכְרִיָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: Yet I had planted thee a noble vine, wholly a right seed: how then art thou turned into the degenerate plant of a strange vine unto me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si laveris te nitro,  et multiplicaveris tibi herbam borith,  maculata es in iniquitate tua coram me,  dicit Dominus Deus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י אִם־תְּכַבְּסִי֙ בַּנֶּ֔תֶר וְתַרְבִּי־לָ֖ךְ בֹּרִ֑ית נִכְתָּ֤ם עֲוֺנֵךְ֙ לְפָנַ֔י נְאֻ֖ם אֲדֹנָ֥י יֱהֹוִֽה׃
verses.king_james: For though thou wash thee with nitre, and take thee much soap, yet thine iniquity is marked before me, saith the Lord God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo dicis : Non sum polluta ;  post Baalim non ambulavi ?  Vide vias tuas in convalle ;  scito quid feceris :  cursor levis explicans vias suas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֣יךְ תֹּאמְרִ֞י לֹ֣א נִטְמֵ֗אתִי אַחֲרֵ֤י הַבְּעָלִים֙ לֹ֣א הָלַ֔כְתִּי רְאִ֤י דַרְכֵּךְ֙ בַּגַּ֔יְא דְּעִ֖י מֶ֣ה עָשִׂ֑ית בִּכְרָ֥ה קַלָּ֖ה מְשָׂרֶ֥כֶת דְּרָכֶֽיהָ׃
verses.king_james: How canst thou say, I am not polluted, I have not gone after Baalim? see thy way in the valley, know what thou hast done: thou art a swift dromedary traversing her ways;

verses.latin_vulgate: Onager assuetus in solitudine,  in desiderio animæ suæ attraxit ventum amoris sui :  nullus avertet eam :  omnes qui quærunt eam non deficient :  in menstruis ejus invenient eam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פֶּ֣רֶה ׀ לִמֻּ֣ד מִדְבָּ֗ר בְּאַוַּ֤ת (נפשו) [נַפְשָׁהּ֙] שָׁאֲפָ֣ה ר֔וּחַ תַּאֲנָתָ֖הּ מִ֣י יְשִׁיבֶ֑נָּה כׇּל־מְבַקְשֶׁ֙יהָ֙ לֹ֣א יִיעָ֔פוּ בְּחׇדְשָׁ֖הּ יִמְצָאֽוּנְהָ׃
verses.king_james: A wild ass used to the wilderness, that snuffeth up the wind at her pleasure; in her occasion who can turn her away? all they that seek her will not weary themselves; in her month they shall find her.

verses.latin_vulgate: Prohibe pedem tuum a nuditate,  et guttur tuum a siti.  Et dixisti : Desperavi : nequaquam faciam :  adamavi quippe alienos,  et post eos ambulabo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִנְעִ֤י רַגְלֵךְ֙ מִיָּחֵ֔ף (וגורנך) [וּגְרוֹנֵ֖ךְ] מִצִּמְאָ֑ה וַתֹּאמְרִ֣י נוֹאָ֔שׁ ל֕וֹא כִּֽי־אָהַ֥בְתִּי זָרִ֖ים וְאַחֲרֵיהֶ֥ם אֵלֵֽךְ׃
verses.king_james: Withhold thy foot from being unshod, and thy throat from thirst: but thou saidst, There is no hope: no; for I have loved strangers, and after them will I go.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo confunditur fur quando deprehenditur,  sic confusi sunt domus Israël,  ipsi et reges eorum,  principes, et sacerdotes, et prophetæ eorum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּבֹ֤שֶׁת גַּנָּב֙ כִּ֣י יִמָּצֵ֔א כֵּ֥ן הֹבִ֖ישׁוּ בֵּ֣ית יִשְׂרָאֵ֑ל הֵ֤מָּה מַלְכֵיהֶם֙ שָׂרֵיהֶ֔ם וְכֹהֲנֵיהֶ֖ם וּנְבִיאֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: As the thief is ashamed when he is found, so is the house of Israel ashamed; they, their kings, their princes, and their priests, and their prophets,

verses.latin_vulgate: dicentes ligno : Pater meus es tu :  et lapidi : Tu me genuisti.  Verterunt ad me tergum et non faciem,  et in tempore afflictionis suæ dicent :  Surge, et libera nos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֹמְרִ֨ים לָעֵ֜ץ אָ֣בִי אַ֗תָּה וְלָאֶ֙בֶן֙ אַ֣תְּ (ילדתני) [יְלִדְתָּ֔נוּ] כִּֽי־פָנ֥וּ אֵלַ֛י עֹ֖רֶף וְלֹ֣א פָנִ֑ים וּבְעֵ֤ת רָעָתָם֙ יֹֽאמְר֔וּ ק֖וּמָה וְהוֹשִׁיעֵֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Saying to a stock, Thou art my father; and to a stone, Thou hast brought me forth: for they have turned their back unto me, and not their face: but in the time of their trouble they will say, Arise, and save us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi sunt dii tui quos fecisti tibi ?  surgant, et liberent te in tempore afflictionis tuæ :  secundum numerum quippe civitatum tuarum erant dii tui, Juda.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַיֵּ֤ה אֱלֹהֶ֙יךָ֙ אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשִׂ֣יתָ לָּ֔ךְ יָק֕וּמוּ אִם־יוֹשִׁיע֖וּךָ בְּעֵ֣ת רָעָתֶ֑ךָ כִּ֚י מִסְפַּ֣ר עָרֶ֔יךָ הָי֥וּ אֱלֹהֶ֖יךָ יְהוּדָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: But where are thy gods that thou hast made thee? let them arise, if they can save thee in the time of thy trouble: for according to the number of thy cities are thy gods, O Judah.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid vultis mecum judicio contendere ?  omnes dereliquistis me, dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֥מָּה תָרִ֖יבוּ אֵלָ֑י כֻּלְּכֶ֛ם פְּשַׁעְתֶּ֥ם בִּ֖י נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore will ye plead with me? ye all have transgressed against me, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Frustra percussi filios vestros :  disciplinam non receperunt.  Devoravit gladius vester prophetas vestros :  quasi leo vastator
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַשָּׁוְא֙ הִכֵּ֣יתִי אֶת־בְּנֵיכֶ֔ם מוּסָ֖ר לֹ֣א לָקָ֑חוּ אָכְלָ֧ה חַרְבְּכֶ֛ם נְבִיאֵיכֶ֖ם כְּאַרְיֵ֥ה מַשְׁחִֽית׃
verses.king_james: In vain have I smitten your children; they received no correction: your own sword hath devoured your prophets, like a destroying lion.

verses.latin_vulgate: generatio vestra.  Videte verbum Domini :  numquid solitudo factus sum Israëli,  aut terra serotina ?  quare ergo dixit populus meus : Recessimus ;  non veniemus ultra ad te ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַדּ֗וֹר אַתֶּם֙ רְא֣וּ דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֔ה הֲמִדְבָּ֤ר הָיִ֙יתִי֙ לְיִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אִ֛ם אֶ֥רֶץ מַאְפֵּ֖לְיָ֑ה מַדּ֜וּעַ אָמְר֤וּ עַמִּי֙ רַ֔דְנוּ לֽוֹא־נָב֥וֹא ע֖וֹד אֵלֶֽיךָ׃
verses.king_james: O generation, see ye the word of the Lord. Have I been a wilderness unto Israel? a land of darkness? wherefore say my people, We are lords; we will come no more unto thee?

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid obliviscetur virgo ornamenti sui,  aut sponsa fasciæ pectoralis suæ ?  populus vero meus oblitus est mei diebus innumeris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲתִשְׁכַּ֤ח בְּתוּלָה֙ עֶדְיָ֔הּ כַּלָּ֖ה קִשֻּׁרֶ֑יהָ וְעַמִּ֣י שְׁכֵח֔וּנִי יָמִ֖ים אֵ֥ין מִסְפָּֽר׃
verses.king_james: Can a maid forget her ornaments, or a bride her attire? yet my people have forgotten me days without number.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid niteris bonam ostendere viam tuam  ad quærendam dilectionem,  quæ insuper et malitias tuas docuisti vias tuas,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־תֵּיטִ֥בִי דַרְכֵּ֖ךְ לְבַקֵּ֣שׁ אַהֲבָ֑ה לָכֵן֙ גַּ֣ם אֶת־הָרָע֔וֹת (למדתי) [לִמַּ֖דְתְּ] אֶת־דְּרָכָֽיִךְ׃
verses.king_james: Why trimmest thou thy way to seek love? therefore hast thou also taught the wicked ones thy ways.

verses.latin_vulgate: et in alis tuis inventus est sanguis animarum pauperum et innocentum ?  non in fossis inveni eos,  sed in omnibus quæ supra memoravi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֤ם בִּכְנָפַ֙יִךְ֙ נִמְצְא֔וּ דַּ֛ם נַפְשׁ֥וֹת אֶבְיוֹנִ֖ים נְקִיִּ֑ים לֹֽא־בַמַּחְתֶּ֥רֶת מְצָאתִ֖ים כִּ֥י עַל־כׇּל־אֵֽלֶּה׃
verses.king_james: Also in thy skirts is found the blood of the souls of the poor innocents: I have not found it by secret search, but upon all these.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixisti : Absque peccato et innocens ego sum,  et propterea avertatur furor tuus a me.  Ecce ego judicio contendam tecum,  eo quod dixeris : Non peccavi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַתֹּֽאמְרִי֙ כִּ֣י נִקֵּ֔יתִי אַ֛ךְ שָׁ֥ב אַפּ֖וֹ מִמֶּ֑נִּי הִנְנִי֙ נִשְׁפָּ֣ט אוֹתָ֔ךְ עַל־אׇמְרֵ֖ךְ לֹ֥א חָטָֽאתִי׃
verses.king_james: Yet thou sayest, Because I am innocent, surely his anger shall turn from me. Behold, I will plead with thee, because thou sayest, I have not sinned.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quam vilis facta es nimis, iterans vias tuas !  et ab Ægypto confunderis,  sicut confusa es ab Assur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־תֵּזְלִ֥י מְאֹ֖ד לְשַׁנּ֣וֹת אֶת־דַּרְכֵּ֑ךְ גַּ֤ם מִמִּצְרַ֙יִם֙ תֵּבֹ֔שִׁי כַּאֲשֶׁר־בֹּ֖שְׁתְּ מֵאַשּֽׁוּר׃
verses.king_james: Why gaddest thou about so much to change thy way? thou also shalt be ashamed of Egypt, as thou wast ashamed of Assyria.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nam et ab ista egredieris,  et manus tuæ erunt super caput tuum :  quoniam obtrivit Dominus confidentiam tuam,  et nihil habebis prosperum in ea.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: גַּ֣ם מֵאֵ֥ת זֶה֙ תֵּֽצְאִ֔י וְיָדַ֖יִךְ עַל־רֹאשֵׁ֑ךְ כִּֽי־מָאַ֤ס יְהֹוָה֙ בְּמִבְטַחַ֔יִךְ וְלֹ֥א תַצְלִ֖יחִי לָהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Yea, thou shalt go forth from him, and thine hands upon thine head: for the Lord hath rejected thy confidences, and thou shalt not prosper in them.
